operating_state: Total sales and revenues increased 22% to $17.318 billion in Q2 2023 and 19% to $33.2 billion for the six-month period, driven by favorable price realization and higher sales volume across all three primary segments. Sales volume growth reflected higher equipment sales to end users and dealer inventory increases, particularly in Energy & Transportation, contrasting with inventory decreases in the prior year period. Order backlog remained stable at approximately $30.7 billion at quarter-end. Regional performance was broadly positive with double-digit growth in North America and EAME, while Asia/Pacific and Latin America posted moderate increases.
cost_structure: Operating profit margin expanded significantly to 21.1% in Q2 and 19.2% for the six-month period compared to 13.6% in the prior year, driven by favorable price realization and higher sales volume partially offset by increased manufacturing costs. Manufacturing cost pressures primarily reflected higher material costs, while increased expenses were driven by strategic investments in services growth, digital capabilities, electrification, and autonomy initiatives, plus unfavorable deferred compensation plan adjustments.
material_events: The company completed the divestiture of its Longwall business in Q1 2023, resulting in a pre-tax loss primarily from accumulated foreign currency translation release. The Board approved an 8% quarterly dividend increase to $1.30 per share in June 2023, while share repurchases totaled $1.83 billion in the first six months with $11.0 billion remaining under authorization. Operating cash flow reached $4.82 billion for the six-month period, up $2.28 billion year-over-year.
macro_exposures: The company continues monitoring supply chain disruptions, inflationary cost pressures, and labor constraints across components, transportation, and raw materials requiring ongoing contingency planning adjustments. International trade policies, tariff changes, currency fluctuations, and geopolitical instability present ongoing risks to competitive positioning and demand patterns, with Asia/Pacific sales affected by regional currency weakness and commodity price fluctuations remaining considerations for business operations.
forward_outlook: Management expects strong full-year 2023 top-line performance supported by price realization and higher equipment sales to end users, with dealer inventory slightly higher at year-end versus 2022. Price realization benefits are expected to moderate in the second half as prior increases lap, while strategic investments in services and sustainability initiatives will continue. The company anticipates higher pension expense due to elevated interest rates.
uncertainty_profile: The company expects approximately $700 million in total 2023 restructuring costs, including the completed Longwall divestiture impact and additional strategic product rationalization actions. Financial covenant compliance continues with consolidated net worth at $18.30 billion versus $9.00 billion minimum requirement, while credit rating agencies maintain mid-A ratings with Fitch upgrading to high-A in May 2023.
